name: Orbit Bridge Multisig Drain
tldr: On New Year's Day 2024 an attacker controlling 7 of 10 Orbit Bridge multisig signer keys forged valid withdrawal authorizations and drained $81.5M in ETH, DAI, USDC, USDT, and wBTC from the Ethereum-side vault. The attack is attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group.
coreContradiction: The bridge's 5-of-7 signing threshold was meant to distribute trust, but attacker control of 7 signers turned the quorum into a single point of failure.

attackSteps.label: Signer keys stolen
attackSteps.detail: Attacker (suspected Lazarus Group) obtained private keys for 7 of the 10 Orbit Bridge multisig signers, likely via social engineering or the deliberately weakened firewall policies set by Ozys' former CISO in November 2023.

attackSteps.label: Tornado Cash funding
attackSteps.detail: Attacker funded the exploit wallet via Tornado Cash through intermediary address 0x70462bFB204BF3CcB0560f259072F8E3A85b3512 to obscure the trail.

attackSteps.label: Forge withdrawal messages
attackSteps.detail: Using 7 compromised signer keys, the attacker produced valid multisig-signed withdrawal messages to the Orbit Bridge Ethereum vault, satisfying the signing threshold.

attackSteps.label: Drain DAI (10M)
attackSteps.detail: First withdrawal: 10,000,000 DAI transferred from the ETH vault to the attacker at 21:08 UTC on December 31 / January 1.

attackSteps.label: Drain WBTC (231)
attackSteps.detail: 231 WBTC (~$9.8M) drained from the ETH vault in the second withdrawal transaction.

attackSteps.label: Drain ETH (9,500)
attackSteps.detail: 9,500 ETH (~$21.5M) drained from the ETH vault.

attackSteps.label: Drain USDC (10M)
attackSteps.detail: 10,000,000 USDC drained from the ETH vault.

attackSteps.label: Drain USDT (30M)
attackSteps.detail: 30,000,000 USDT drained from the ETH vault at 21:25 UTC, completing the five-asset drain totaling $81.5M.
